tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Strait of Hormuz effectively closed post-Feb 28 US-Israeli strikes on Iran. Brent now at $85.60/bbl (Day 7, Mar 6, +3.0% on session) — new crisis high driven by Iraq output cut of ~1.5M bbl/day (storage/export constraints) with Kuwait and UAE potentially next. Goldman Sachs models $18/bbl 'Iran shock' add-on; BloombergNEF targets $91/bbl under prolonged disruption; J.P. Morgan upper-end $130. ASEAN energy producers (PTTEP, Medco Energi, Sapura Energy) benefit directly from price spike. 23% of global LNG trade transits Hormuz — Qatar's 77 mtpa effectively offline via force majeure (min. 1 month recovery). Long near-dated Brent vs short deferred as backwardation steepens. Entered at $79; now $85.60; base target $106, stress target $125. US sub sank Iranian frigate Iris Dena off Sri Lanka — war expanding to Indian Ocean raises tail risk further.

tradeIdeas.title: Long ASEAN Banks / Short ASEAN Energy Equities
tradeIdeas.rationale: ASEAN Banks index at 208.1 (-2.5% on Mar 6) — accelerating selloff as credit widens and Indian Ocean naval expansion raises systemic risk; net-oil-exporting names (Malaysia, Singapore banks) relatively insulated. ASEAN Energy Equities at 140.5 (+2.4%) bid strongly on Iraq output cut and Brent at $85.60. iTraxx Asia IG at 145bp (+7bp on Mar 6) signals credit still widening; ASEAN HY at 460bp (+16bp). MUFG flags MYR and SGD bank balance sheets as relatively insulated vs PHP/IDR. Recommend maintaining bank long for rate-cut delay beneficiaries. Wilmar and IJM plantation equity longs complement as oil-correlated ASEAN plays.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short — Elevated Conviction on Iran Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: PHP most vulnerable ASEAN currency per MUFG (Michael Wan, Mar 3). USD/PHP at 59.10 (Mar 6, +0.8% on session) — approaching the 59.50 strike breakeven. 1,400+ OFWs requesting repatriation adding remittance income uncertainty; Philippines formally rejected EDCA-base link to Middle East operations, creating US alliance friction. >50% of Philippines power generation relies on imported fossil fuels; no strategic LNG reserves. Philippines current account deficit widens to -4.5% of GDP at $90/bbl (now likely at $92–95 intraday). BSP rate cut cycle now on hold indefinitely. JKM LNG at $17.80 (+6.0%) compounds LNG import cost exposure. MUFG stress target: 61.00 on sustained oil spike above $100. Stop-loss at 57.50.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short — Dual Exposure to Iran Shock and Shadow Fleet Risk
tradeIdeas.rationale: IDR at 17,050 (Day 7, Mar 6, +0.8% on session). Net oil importer exposed to Hormuz-driven price surge with Brent now at $85.60 and Iraq-driven supply shortfall compounding. Bank Indonesia forced into defensive rate hold at 4.75% despite growth pressure; Indonesia 10Y at 7.40% (+4bp). Iraq output cut of ~1.5M bbl/day forces Indonesia to seek dearer US/Australian alternatives — crude import cost inflation accelerating. Indonesian-branded crude surging into China (~291,000 bpd) covering Iranian origin — OFAC enforcement tightening. Indian Ocean expansion of the war (Iris Dena sinking off Sri Lanka) raises tail risk for Indonesian crude rerouting via Cape of Good Hope. Target 17,500–18,000 in stress scenario; 19,000 in tail. Stress target $100 Brent is now within a 17% move of current levels.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection — Hold Position
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG at 145bp (+7bp on Mar 6, accelerating wider). Iran shock + US naval expansion to Indian Ocean (Iris Dena sinking) + Iraq supply cut creates triple-front credit deterioration. CDS entered at 102bp on pre-shock level — now deep in-the-money at +43bp. P&I blackout fully in effect; 329 tankers trapped in Gulf; war escalation confirmed by Hegseth ('accelerating, not decelerating'). Hold existing 5Y position; 180–220bp is credible stress target in 1–3 month Hormuz disruption scenario. Sapura Energy and Hyflux bonds are the most vulnerable ASEAN HY names. ASEAN HY Composite at 460bp (+16bp on Mar 6) accelerating wider.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF — Spread Re-widening on Hormuz Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM at $17.80/MMBtu (+6.0% on Mar 6) as Iraq output cut of ~1.5M bbl/day compounds Qatar force majeure at Ras Laffan (min. 1-month recovery). TTF at €55.40/MWh (+4.9% on Mar 6) — Atlantic Basin LNG re-routing has structural limits; European storage drawdown risk rising. 84% of Hormuz LNG goes to Asia; JKM/TTF spread continuing to widen on Day 7, confirming Asian supply squeeze is structural. IRGC threat to close Strait of Malacca (retaliation for Iris Dena sinking) represents an extreme upside risk to JKM — if Malacca is disrupted, Australian LNG exports to NE Asia are also at risk. Wilmar (palm oil / biodiesel competitor to LNG) and PTTEP (LNG producer beneficiary) are key client-linked expressions of this theme. In 1–3 month Hormuz closure + Iraq cuts, JKM could approach $28–30/MMBtu.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: CRITICAL: Sapura Energy's MYR debt maturities 2026–2027 represent an acute refinancing risk. iTraxx Asia IG at 145bp and ASEAN HY at 460bp signal the window for secured bank refinancing is closing fast — each day of war escalation reprices bilateral bank appetite. E&P capex clients recovering above $85.60/bbl Brent (Day 7), but capex lag means Sapura revenue uplift is 2–3 quarters behind oil price. High leverage (net debt ~MYR 10bn), MAS February 2026 FATF guidance, and P&I blackout now fully in effect create compounding risk. Engage bilateral bank discussions this week; window may close entirely if iTraxx breaches 160bp.

THB bond maturity H2 2026 — BOT rate hold (inflation pressure from Brent at $85.60) means pre-financing at current 3.26% TH 10Y is the recommended window. Window is narrowing: THB has weakened to 32.10 (vs 31.70 yesterday) adding FX cost; if BOT reprices for oil-driven inflation, 3.40%+ is plausible. PTTEP's upstream EBITDA improves materially with Brent at $85.60 — use the revenue tailwind to justify pre-financing now. MAS February 2026 FATF guidance on Iran sanctions may affect Gulf of Oman JV compliance — complete jurisdictional review in parallel. Indian Ocean naval expansion (Iris Dena sinking off Sri Lanka) adds strategic shipping risk for PTTEP's LNG and crude offtake routes.

Wilmar's USD revolving credit facility should be expanded immediately to accommodate oil-driven input cost inflation. Bunker fuel represents ~12% of cost base — at $85.60 Brent, the annualised fuel cost delta vs Q4 2025 is est. USD 390–450M. Biodiesel mandate tailwind (Indonesia B40, Malaysia B20) improves EBITDA but cash conversion lag creates acute working capital pressure. Pre-emptive RCF expansion of USD 500–750M recommended — iTraxx Asia IG at 145bp is still executable for investment-grade borrowers but closing. Indian Ocean naval expansion (Iris Dena sinking off Sri Lanka) creates new tail risk for Wilmar's palm oil/oilseed shipping routes — logistics contingency review recommended.

Energy cost relief window has narrowed further: Base scenario probability has fallen to 30% (from 38% on Mar 4) as war escalates to Indian Ocean. If Hormuz de-escalates, Brent could retrace toward $68–72 — creating a brief refinancing window. However, at current $85.60 Brent and ASEAN HY at 460bp, capital markets are effectively closed for unsecured debt. Explore alternative private credit, DFI, or multilateral bridge financing as the only viable near-term path. Engage restructuring advisor immediately to model oil-path-contingent scenarios. Energy cost covenant must be reviewed vs restructuring plan assumptions made at $55–60/bbl — the $25/bbl breach is structurally material.
